finding: The bulk-closure manifest demanded by `ATM_TASK_AUDIT_BULK_CLOSE_WITHOUT_MANIFEST` has no governed committable home: `.atm/history/evidence/` placement is blocked by protected-state rules (plain commit and `git record-commit` both fail with `evidence-file-missing-task-context`, record-commit additionally with `ATM_COMMIT_SESSION_MISSING`), while the alternative accepted path `atomic_workbench/evidence/` is gitignored. The only working state is an untracked local file, so the attestation that unblocks pre-push is not durable and every fresh clone or teammate hits the same block again.
expectedBehavior: Provide a governed lane to commit audit-required attestations (tracked manifest location or a record-only lane that accepts them with attribution), so passing the audit leaves a durable, shared artifact.
evidenceOrRepro: 2026-07-11 while pushing main: manifest in `.atm/history/evidence/` blocked twice; moving it to `atomic_workbench/evidence/` satisfied `hasBulkClosureManifest` from disk and the push succeeded, but the file remains untracked.
followUp: Decide the canonical tracked home for bulk closure manifests and teach pre-commit to accept it with actor attribution.
